services.name: Twitter / X
services.description: Post tweets, search content, track analytics, and manage your X presence through automated agents.

configFields.label: Client ID
configFields.helpText: Found in your X Developer Portal under Keys and Tokens

steps: Go to developer.x.com and sign in with your X account.
Create a new Project and App in the Developer Portal.
Navigate to Keys and Tokens and generate your API credentials.
Under User Authentication Settings, enable OAuth 2.0.
Set the callback URL to http://localhost:3000/api/field-ops/callback/twitter.
Copy the Client ID, Client Secret, Access Token, and Access Token Secret into the fields below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free tier: 1,500 tweets/month read. Basic ($200/mo): 3,000 tweets/month post.

services.name: LinkedIn
services.description: Manage your LinkedIn presence, post updates, engage with your professional network, and track content performance.

steps: Go to linkedin.com/developers and sign in.
Create a new Application under My Apps.
Request the w_member_social and r_liteprofile OAuth scopes.
Under Auth tab, add http://localhost:3000/api/field-ops/callback/linkedin as a redirect URL.
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the Auth tab.
Generate an access token using the OAuth 2.0 flow or LinkedIn's token tool.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for personal profiles. Company pages require LinkedIn Marketing API access.

services.name: Facebook Pages
services.description: Manage Facebook business pages, publish posts, moderate comments, and track page insights.

steps: Go to developers.facebook.com and create a new App (Business type).
Add the Facebook Login and Pages API products to your app.
In Settings > Basic, copy your App ID and App Secret.
Use the Graph API Explorer to generate a Page Access Token.
Exchange the short-lived token for a long-lived token using the token exchange endpoint.
Copy your credentials into the fields below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free. Requires a Facebook Page and verified Business account for production.

services.name: Instagram
services.description: Track Instagram engagement, analyze post performance, and manage your visual content strategy.

steps: Convert your Instagram account to a Business or Creator account.
Link it to a Facebook Page in Instagram Settings.
Create a Facebook App at developers.facebook.com (same as Facebook setup).
Add the Instagram Graph API product to your app.
Use the Graph API Explorer to get your Business Account ID and access token.
Copy the credentials into the fields below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free. Requires Instagram Business/Creator account linked to Facebook Page.

services.name: Bluesky
services.description: Post to Bluesky, manage your feed, interact with the AT Protocol social network.

configFields.label: App Password
configFields.helpText: Generate an App Password in Bluesky Settings > App Passwords

steps: Open Bluesky and go to Settings > App Passwords.
Click Add App Password and give it a name (e.g., Mission Control).
Copy the generated app password (you won't see it again).
Enter your Bluesky handle and the app password below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free. Bluesky is a free, open social network.

services.name: Ayrshare (Multi-Platform)
services.description: Post to 8+ social platforms simultaneously including X, Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, Reddit, and Pinterest.

steps: Sign up at ayrshare.com and create an account.
In the Ayrshare dashboard, link your social media accounts (X, Facebook, etc.).
Go to API Keys in your Ayrshare dashboard.
Generate a new API key and copy it.
Paste the API key below. All linked social accounts will be available.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 1 profile, limited posts. Premium ($29/mo): 5 profiles, unlimited posts.

services.name: Gmail
services.description: Read, send, and manage emails through Gmail. Handle labels, attachments, and automate email workflows.

steps: Go to console.cloud.google.com → create a new project (or select existing).
Enable the Gmail API: APIs & Services → Library → search 'Gmail API' → Enable.

Configure OAuth consent screen: APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen → App name, support email, scopes (add https://mail.google.com/). Click 'Publish App' under Audience to make refresh tokens long-lived (otherwise they expire every 7 days).
Create OAuth 2.0 credentials: APIs & Services → Credentials → Create Credentials → OAuth Client ID → Web application.
Add TWO Authorized redirect URIs: (1) http://localhost:3000/api/field-ops/callback/gmail and (2) https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground — then Save.
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the credentials page.
Open the OAuth Playground: developers.google.com/oauthplayground. Click the gear icon (top-right) → check 'Use your own OAuth credentials' → paste your Client ID and Client Secret.

In Step 1 of the Playground: select 'Gmail API v1' → check 'https://mail.google.com/' → click 'Authorize APIs'. If you see 'Google hasn't verified this app', click Advanced → 'Go to [app name] (unsafe)' — this is safe since you are the developer.
In Step 2: click 'Exchange authorization code for tokens'. Copy the Refresh Token shown (you do NOT need Step 3).
In Mission Control → Services → Gmail → enter the Client ID, Client Secret, and Refresh Token → enter your vault master password → Activate.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for personal Gmail. Google Workspace plans start at $7/user/month.

services.name: Slack
services.description: Send messages to Slack channels, manage threads, share files, and automate team communication.

configFields.label: Bot Token
configFields.helpText: Start with xoxb- prefix. Found in your Slack App settings under OAuth & Permissions.

configFields.label: Default Channel
configFields.helpText: Optional default channel for messages

steps: Go to api.slack.com/apps and click Create New App.
Choose From scratch and select your workspace.
Under OAuth & Permissions, add Bot Token Scopes: chat:write, channels:read, files:write.
Click Install to Workspace and authorize.
Copy the Bot User OAuth Token (starts with xoxb-).
Invite the bot to your desired channels with /invite @botname.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for basic usage. Slack Pro ($8.75/user/mo) for full message history.

services.name: Discord
services.description: Automate Discord bot interactions, send messages, moderate communities, and manage server engagement.

configFields.label: Server (Guild) ID
configFields.helpText: Right-click your server name > Copy Server ID (enable Developer Mode first)

steps: Go to discord.com/developers/applications and click New Application.
Under Bot tab, click Reset Token and copy the bot token.
Enable the Message Content Intent under Privileged Gateway Intents.
Under OAuth2 > URL Generator, select bot scope and desired permissions.
Copy the generated URL and open it to invite the bot to your server.
Enable Developer Mode in Discord (Settings > Advanced) to copy server/channel IDs.
setupGuide.pricing: Free. Discord bots are free to create and host.

services.name: YouTube
services.description: Manage YouTube videos, upload Shorts, track analytics, read transcripts, and manage channel content.

configFields.label: Refresh Token
configFields.placeholder: OAuth 2.0 Refresh Token for YouTube scopes

steps: Go to console.cloud.google.com and enable the YouTube Data API v3.
Create OAuth 2.0 credentials under APIs & Services > Credentials.
Set redirect URI to http://localhost:3000/api/field-ops/callback/youtube.
Use the OAuth Playground to authorize with YouTube scopes (youtube.upload, youtube.readonly).
Copy the refresh token, Client ID, and Client Secret.
Your YouTube channel must be in good standing for uploads.
setupGuide.pricing: Free. YouTube API has daily quota limits (10,000 units/day default).

services.name: WordPress
services.description: Publish blog posts, manage pages, handle media uploads, and manage your WordPress site content.

configFields.label: Site URL
configFields.helpText: Your WordPress site URL (must have REST API enabled)

configFields.label: Application Password
configFields.helpText: Generate in WordPress admin: Users > Profile > Application Passwords

steps: Ensure your WordPress site is version 5.6+ (has Application Passwords built in).
Log into your WordPress admin panel.
Go to Users > Profile > Application Passwords section.
Enter a name (e.g., Mission Control) and click Add New Application Password.
Copy the generated password immediately (it won't be shown again).
Enter your site URL, username, and the application password below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free (self-hosted). WordPress.com Business plan ($33/mo) for REST API access.

services.name: Figma
services.description: Extract design data, inspect components, access design tokens, and bridge design-to-code workflows.

configFields.label: Personal Access Token
configFields.helpText: Generate in Figma: Settings > Account > Personal access tokens

steps: Log into Figma at figma.com.
Go to Settings > Account > Personal access tokens.
Click Generate new token, give it a description (e.g., Mission Control).
Set appropriate scopes (File read-only is sufficient for design extraction).
Copy the token immediately and paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for viewing. Figma Professional ($15/editor/mo) for full API access.

services.name: Canva
services.description: Access Canva's design tools, create graphics, and integrate design workflows with your business processes.

steps: Go to canva.dev and sign up for a Canva Connect developer account.
Create a new integration in the developer portal.
Configure the required scopes for your use case.
Generate API credentials and copy the API key.
Paste the API key below.
setupGuide.pricing: Canva Free available. Canva Pro ($15/mo) for brand kit and premium features.

services.name: DALL-E / Image Generation
services.description: Generate images using DALL-E 3 and other AI models. Create marketing visuals, logos, social media graphics, and more.

configFields.label: OpenAI API Key
configFields.helpText: Get your API key from platform.openai.com/api-keys

steps: Go to platform.openai.com and sign in (or create an account).
Navigate to API Keys section.
Click Create new secret key and give it a name.
Copy the key immediately (you won't see it again).
Add billing information if you haven't already (required for API usage).
Paste the API key below.
setupGuide.pricing: DALL-E 3: $0.040/image (1024x1024). DALL-E 2: $0.020/image. Pay-per-use.

services.name: Stripe
services.description: Review payments, manage refunds, handle invoices, resolve disputes, and monitor your revenue through Stripe.

configFields.label: Secret Key
configFields.helpText: Use test keys (sk_test_) for development, live keys (sk_live_) for production

configFields.label: Mode
configFields.helpText: Start with test mode to verify everything works safely

steps: Log into dashboard.stripe.com.
Go to Developers > API Keys.
For testing: Copy the Secret key from Test mode (starts with sk_test_).
For production: Copy the Secret key from Live mode (starts with sk_live_).
IMPORTANT: Start with test mode keys. Only use live keys after thorough testing.
Paste the key and select the appropriate mode below.
setupGuide.pricing: 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction. No monthly fees. Free test mode.

services.name: Shopify
services.description: Manage orders, track shipments, process cancellations, and monitor your Shopify store operations.

configFields.label: Admin API Access Token
configFields.helpText: Generate in Shopify Admin: Settings > Apps > Develop apps

steps: Log into your Shopify admin panel.
Go to Settings > Apps and sales channels > Develop apps.
Click Create an app and name it (e.g., Mission Control).
Configure Admin API scopes (read_orders, write_orders, read_products, etc.).
Click Install app and confirm.
Copy the Admin API access token (starts with shpat_) and your shop domain.
setupGuide.pricing: Requires active Shopify plan. Basic: $39/mo. Shopify: $105/mo.

services.name: Google Search Console
services.description: Monitor search performance, track keyword rankings, check indexing status, and analyze Core Web Vitals.

configFields.label: Site URL
configFields.helpText: Must match a verified property in Search Console

steps: Verify your site in Google Search Console at search.google.com/search-console.
Go to console.cloud.google.com and enable the Search Console API.
Create OAuth 2.0 credentials (same project as Gmail/YouTube if using those).
Use the OAuth Playground to generate a refresh token with Search Console scopes.
Copy your credentials and verified site URL below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free. Google Search Console is completely free.

services.name: Google Analytics 4
services.description: Access web analytics data, track user engagement, monitor conversions, and generate performance reports.

configFields.label: GA4 Property ID
configFields.helpText: Found in GA4: Admin > Property Settings

steps: Set up GA4 on your website at analytics.google.com.
Note your Property ID from Admin > Property Settings.
Go to console.cloud.google.com and enable the Google Analytics Data API.
Create or reuse OAuth 2.0 credentials.
Use the OAuth Playground to get a refresh token with Analytics scopes.
Copy your credentials and Property ID below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for standard GA4. GA4 360 (enterprise) is paid.

services.name: SEMrush
services.description: Access keyword research, domain analytics, backlink data, and competitive intelligence for SEO strategy.

configFields.label: SEMrush API Key
configFields.helpText: Found in SEMrush: Settings > Subscription Info > API Key

steps: Log into your SEMrush account at semrush.com.
Go to Settings (gear icon) > Subscription Info.
Find your API Key in the API section.
Copy the API key and paste it below.
Note: API calls consume API units from your plan.
setupGuide.pricing: Pro ($139.95/mo): 10,000 API units. Guru ($249.95/mo): 30,000 API units.

services.name: Ahrefs
services.description: Access SEO data including backlink profiles, keyword rankings, traffic estimates, and content gap analysis.

steps: Log into ahrefs.com with your account.
Go to your Account settings.
Navigate to the API section.
Generate or copy your API token.
Paste the token below.
setupGuide.pricing: Lite ($129/mo): 500 API rows/mo. Standard ($249/mo): higher limits.

services.name: HubSpot
services.description: Manage contacts, track deals, log emails, and automate your sales and marketing workflows through HubSpot CRM.

configFields.label: Private App Access Token
configFields.helpText: Create a Private App in HubSpot: Settings > Integrations > Private Apps

steps: Log into app.hubspot.com.
Go to Settings > Integrations > Private Apps.
Click Create a private app.
Name it (e.g., Mission Control) and select required scopes (crm.objects.contacts, deals, etc.).
Click Create app and copy the access token.
Paste the token below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free CRM available. Starter ($20/mo), Professional ($890/mo) for automation.

services.name: Pipedrive
services.description: Access leads, manage deals, track pipelines, and organize sales activities through Pipedrive CRM.

configFields.label: API Token
configFields.helpText: Found in Pipedrive: Settings > Personal Preferences > API

steps: Log into your Pipedrive account.
Go to Settings (gear icon) > Personal Preferences > API.
Copy your personal API token.
Paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Essential ($14.90/user/mo). Advanced ($27.90/user/mo). Professional ($49.90/user/mo).

services.name: Linear
services.description: Manage issues, track projects, coordinate milestones, and streamline engineering workflows.

configFields.label: Linear API Key
configFields.helpText: Generate at linear.app: Settings > API > Personal API Keys

steps: Log into linear.app.
Go to Settings > Account > API.
Click Create key under Personal API Keys.
Give it a label (e.g., Mission Control) and copy the key.
Paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for small teams. Standard ($8/user/mo). Plus ($14/user/mo).

services.name: Notion
services.description: Manage Notion pages, databases, and todos. Organize knowledge bases and collaborative documents.

configFields.label: Integration Token
configFields.helpText: Create an integration at notion.so/my-integrations

steps: Go to notion.so/my-integrations.
Click New integration and name it (e.g., Mission Control).
Select the workspace to connect.
Choose capabilities (Read content, Update content, Insert content).
Copy the Internal Integration Secret (starts with secret_).
In Notion, share the specific pages/databases with your integration (click Share > Invite).
setupGuide.pricing: Free for personal use. Plus ($10/user/mo). Business ($18/user/mo).

services.name: Asana
services.description: Manage tasks, projects, and workflows. Track team progress and coordinate work across Asana.

steps: Log into app.asana.com.
Go to My Settings > Apps > Developer Apps.
Click Create new token.
Name it (e.g., Mission Control) and agree to the API terms.
Copy the token and paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for up to 10 users. Premium ($13.49/user/mo). Business ($30.49/user/mo).

services.name: Trello
services.description: Manage Trello boards, cards, and checklists. Automate kanban-style project workflows.

configFields.label: Token
configFields.helpText: Generate at trello.com/power-ups/admin (click the API Key link)

steps: Go to trello.com/power-ups/admin.
Click on the API Key link to see your API key.
Click the Token link next to your API key to generate a token.
Authorize Mission Control to access your Trello account.
Copy both the API Key and Token and paste them below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for basic boards. Standard ($6/user/mo). Premium ($12.50/user/mo).

services.name: Vercel
services.description: Manage deployments, monitor projects, access analytics, and control environment variables on Vercel.

configFields.label: Vercel Token
configFields.helpText: Generate at vercel.com/account/tokens

steps: Log into vercel.com.
Go to Account Settings > Tokens.
Click Create Token.
Name it (e.g., Mission Control), set scope (Full Account or specific team).
Set expiration (recommend: no expiration for persistent use, or 1 year).
Copy the token and paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Hobby (free). Pro ($20/user/mo). Enterprise (custom).

services.name: Cloudflare
services.description: Manage Workers, DNS records, cache, Pages deployments, and edge compute on Cloudflare.

configFields.label: API Token
configFields.helpText: Create at dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens

configFields.label: Account ID
configFields.helpText: Found on any domain's overview page in the right sidebar

steps: Log into dash.cloudflare.com.
Go to My Profile > API Tokens.
Click Create Token.
Use the Edit zone DNS template or create a custom token with specific permissions.
Copy the token (shown only once).
Find your Account ID on any domain's Overview page (right sidebar).
setupGuide.pricing: Free tier available. Pro ($20/mo/site). Business ($200/mo/site).

services.name: GitHub
services.description: Manage repositories, issues, pull requests, code search, and automate development workflows.

configFields.label: Personal Access Token
configFields.helpText: Generate at github.com/settings/tokens. Use fine-grained tokens for better security.

steps: Go to github.com/settings/tokens.
Click Generate new token (fine-grained recommended).
Set a name, expiration, and select repository access.
Choose permissions: Contents (read/write), Issues (read/write), Pull requests (read/write).
Click Generate token and copy it immediately.
Paste the token below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for public repos. Pro ($4/mo). Team ($4/user/mo). Enterprise ($21/user/mo).

services.name: Supabase
services.description: Execute queries, manage tables, run migrations, access project logs, and manage your Supabase backend.

configFields.label: Project URL
configFields.helpText: Found in Project Settings > API

configFields.label: Service Role Key
configFields.helpText: Found in Project Settings > API. Use service_role key for full access.

steps: Log into supabase.com and open your project.
Go to Project Settings > API.
Copy the Project URL.
Copy the service_role key (secret, gives full DB access).
CAUTION: The service_role key bypasses Row Level Security. Use with care.
Paste both values below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free tier: 500MB DB, 1GB storage. Pro ($25/mo): 8GB DB, 100GB storage.

services.name: Google Drive
services.description: Search, list, upload, and manage files in Google Drive. Access documents and organize cloud storage.

configFields.label: Refresh Token
configFields.placeholder: OAuth 2.0 Refresh Token with Drive scopes

steps: Go to console.cloud.google.com and enable the Google Drive API.
Create or reuse OAuth 2.0 credentials.
Use the OAuth Playground to authorize with Drive scopes (drive.file or drive.readonly).
Tip: Use drive.file scope to limit access to files created by this app.
Copy the refresh token, Client ID, and Client Secret below.
setupGuide.pricing: 15GB free with Google account. Google One: 100GB ($2/mo), 2TB ($10/mo).

services.name: Dropbox
services.description: Manage files and folders, upload content, sync files, and organize your Dropbox cloud storage.

configFields.label: Access Token
configFields.helpText: Generate at dropbox.com/developers/apps

steps: Go to dropbox.com/developers/apps.
Click Create app.
Choose Scoped access and Full Dropbox (or App folder for restricted).
Name your app (e.g., mission-control).
Under Settings > OAuth 2 > Generated access token, click Generate.
Copy the token and paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Basic (free): 2GB. Plus ($11.99/mo): 2TB. Professional ($24/mo): 3TB.

services.name: Google Ads
services.description: Analyze campaign performance, track conversions, manage keywords, and monitor ad spend through Google Ads.

configFields.label: Customer ID
configFields.helpText: Found at the top of your Google Ads dashboard

steps: Log into ads.google.com and note your Customer ID (top right, format: xxx-xxx-xxxx).
Apply for a Developer Token: Tools > Setup > API Center.
Create OAuth 2.0 credentials in Google Cloud Console.
Use the OAuth Playground with Google Ads API scope to get a refresh token.
Copy all credentials below.
Note: Developer token starts in test mode. Apply for basic access for production.
setupGuide.pricing: No API fees. Ad spend is separate (you set your own budget).

services.name: Facebook / Meta Ads
services.description: Manage ad campaigns, optimize budgets, run A/B tests, and analyze performance across Facebook and Instagram.

configFields.label: Access Token
configFields.placeholder: Long-lived User or System User Token

configFields.label: Ad Account ID
configFields.helpText: Found in Meta Business Suite > Ad Account Settings (starts with act_)

steps: Go to business.facebook.com and ensure you have a Business Account.
Create a Facebook App at developers.facebook.com (Business type).
Add the Marketing API product to your app.
Generate a System User Token with ads_management permission.
Find your Ad Account ID in Business Settings > Ad Accounts.
Copy the token and Ad Account ID below.
setupGuide.pricing: No API fees. Ad spend is separate (you set your own budget). Minimum $1/day.

services.name: Zendesk
services.description: Manage support tickets, search the Help Center, track customer issues, and automate support workflows.

configFields.label: Zendesk Subdomain
configFields.helpText: The subdomain in yourcompany.zendesk.com

steps: Log into your Zendesk admin center.
Go to Apps and integrations > APIs > Zendesk API.
Enable Token Access if not already enabled.
Click Add API token, give it a description.
Copy the token (shown only once).
Enter your subdomain, agent email, and API token below.
setupGuide.pricing: Suite Team ($55/agent/mo). Suite Growth ($89/agent/mo). Suite Professional ($115/agent/mo).

services.name: Freshdesk
services.description: Manage support tickets, track customer contacts, handle conversations, and automate support operations.

configFields.label: API Key
configFields.helpText: Found in your Freshdesk profile settings

steps: Log into your Freshdesk account.
Click your profile icon (top right) > Profile Settings.
Find your API Key in the right sidebar.
Copy the API key.
Enter your Freshdesk domain and API key below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: up to 2 agents. Growth ($18/agent/mo). Pro ($59/agent/mo).

services.name: Google Calendar
services.description: Create events, check availability, manage attendees, handle recurring meetings, and organize your schedule.

configFields.label: Refresh Token
configFields.placeholder: OAuth Refresh Token with Calendar scopes

steps: Go to console.cloud.google.com and enable the Google Calendar API.
Create or reuse OAuth 2.0 credentials.
Use the OAuth Playground to authorize with Calendar scopes (calendar.events, calendar.readonly).
Copy the refresh token, Client ID, and Client Secret.
Paste them below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free with Google account. Google Workspace ($7/user/mo) for business features.

services.name: Calendly
services.description: Manage scheduling links, track bookings, and automate meeting coordination.

configFields.label: Personal Access Token
configFields.helpText: Generate at calendly.com/integrations/api_webhooks

steps: Log into calendly.com.
Go to Integrations > API and Webhooks.
Click Generate New Token.
Copy the personal access token.
Paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 1 event type. Standard ($12/user/mo). Teams ($20/user/mo).

services.name: Brave Search
services.description: Perform web searches, local searches, news searches, and access structured search results programmatically.

configFields.label: Brave Search API Key
configFields.helpText: Get your free API key at brave.com/search/api/

steps: Go to brave.com/search/api/ and sign up for a free account.
Select the Free plan (2,000 queries/month) or a paid plan.
Generate an API key in your dashboard.
Copy the key and paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 2,000 queries/month. Base ($5/1000 queries). Pro ($3/1000 queries).

services.name: Firecrawl
services.description: Scrape websites, crawl entire domains, extract structured data, and convert web pages to clean markdown.

configFields.label: Firecrawl API Key
configFields.helpText: Get your API key at firecrawl.dev

steps: Go to firecrawl.dev and create an account.
Navigate to your API Keys in the dashboard.
Generate a new API key.
Copy the key (starts with fc-) and paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 500 credits/month. Hobby ($19/mo): 3,000 credits. Standard ($99/mo): 100,000 credits.

services.name: PDF Reader
services.description: Extract text from PDF documents with OCR support, parallel processing, and high-accuracy content retrieval.

steps: This service runs locally and requires no API keys or configuration.
The MCP server will be installed automatically when activated.
Simply activate the service and it will be ready to process PDFs.
setupGuide.pricing: Free. Runs entirely on your local machine.

services.name: OpenAI API
services.description: Access GPT models and DALL-E image generation. Use for text generation, analysis, and creative AI tasks.

configFields.label: OpenAI API Key
configFields.helpText: Get your API key at platform.openai.com/api-keys

steps: Go to platform.openai.com and sign in (or create an account).
Navigate to API Keys section.
Click Create new secret key.
Copy the key immediately (starts with sk-).
Ensure billing is set up under Settings > Billing.
Paste the API key below.
setupGuide.pricing: Pay-per-use. GPT-4o: ~$2.50/1M input tokens. DALL-E 3: $0.040/image.

services.name: Ethereum / Base Wallet
services.description: Send ETH, transfer USDC, and check wallet balances on Ethereum mainnet or Base L2. Supports MetaMask or direct private key.

configFields.label: Private Key (server-side)
configFields.helpText: For automated execution. Leave blank if using MetaMask only.

configFields.label: Wallet Address
configFields.helpText: Public address for read-only operations (balance checks). Auto-derived from private key if provided.

configFields.label: Network
configFields.helpText: ethereum or base (default: base)

configFields.label: RPC URL (optional)
configFields.helpText: Custom RPC endpoint. Defaults to public RPC for selected network.

steps: Option A (MetaMask): Install MetaMask browser extension. Connect to Base network. Copy your wallet address into the Address field.
Option B (Private Key): Export your private key from MetaMask (Account Details > Export Private Key). Paste it into the Private Key field.
Choose your network: 'base' (recommended, low gas fees) or 'ethereum' (mainnet).
Optionally provide a custom RPC URL for reliability (e.g., from Alchemy, Infura, or QuickNode).
SECURITY: Private keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM in your vault. They never leave your machine.
setupGuide.pricing: Free. Gas fees apply for transactions (Base: ~$0.01, Ethereum: ~$1-20 depending on congestion).

services.name: Reddit
services.description: Post to subreddits, comment, reply, search content, and monitor mentions across Reddit communities.

configFields.label: Client ID
configFields.helpText: Found in your Reddit app preferences under the app name

configFields.label: User Agent
configFields.helpText: A unique user agent string identifying your app

steps: Go to reddit.com/prefs/apps and sign in.
Scroll down and click 'create another app...' or 'are you a developer? create an app...'.
Select 'script' as the app type.
Fill in the name, description, and redirect URI (http://localhost:3000).
Click Create app.
Copy the Client ID (shown under the app name) and Client Secret.
Enter your Reddit username and password below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free API with 60 req/min rate limit.

services.name: TikTok
services.description: Upload videos, track analytics, manage content, and discover trending sounds on TikTok.

configFields.label: Client Key
configFields.helpText: Found in your TikTok Developer Portal app settings

steps: Go to developers.tiktok.com and sign in.
Click Manage apps and create a new application.
Request Content Posting API access and other required scopes.
Wait for app review approval (may take a few days).
Once approved, copy the Client Key and Client Secret from app settings.
Generate an access token via the OAuth 2.0 flow and paste all credentials below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free for basic posting. Content Posting API requires app review.

services.name: Pinterest
services.description: Create pins, manage boards, track analytics, and gain audience insights on Pinterest.

configFields.label: App ID
configFields.helpText: Found in your Pinterest Developer app settings

steps: Go to developers.pinterest.com and sign in.
Create a new app in the developer portal.
Configure required OAuth scopes (boards:read, pins:read, pins:write).
Submit for app review if needed for standard access.
Get an access token via the OAuth 2.0 flow.
Copy the App ID, App Secret, and access token into the fields below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free tier available. Standard access requires app review.

services.name: Telegram Bot
services.description: Send messages, manage channels and groups, create inline keyboards, and share media through a Telegram bot.

configFields.label: Bot Token
configFields.helpText: Get this from @BotFather on Telegram

configFields.label: Default Chat ID
configFields.helpText: Optional. The chat/channel ID to send messages to by default.

steps: Open Telegram and search for @BotFather.
Send /newbot and follow the prompts to create your bot.
Choose a name and username for your bot.
Copy the bot token that BotFather gives you.
Add your bot to your channel or group as an administrator.
Paste the bot token below. Optionally set a default chat ID.
setupGuide.pricing: Completely free. No rate limits for normal usage.

services.name: Resend
services.description: Send transactional emails, manage templates and domains, and handle webhooks with a modern email API.

configFields.label: API Key
configFields.helpText: Get your API key from the Resend dashboard

configFields.label: From Email
configFields.helpText: Must be from a verified domain in Resend

steps: Sign up at resend.com and verify your email.
Go to Domains and add your sending domain.
Add the DNS records (SPF, DKIM) to verify your domain.
Go to API Keys and click Create API Key.
Copy the API key and set your from email and name below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 100 emails/day, 3,000/month. Pro: $20/mo for 50,000/month.

services.name: ConvertKit (Kit)
services.description: Manage email marketing, newsletters, automations, subscriber lists, and sequences through ConvertKit.

configFields.label: API Key
configFields.helpText: Found in ConvertKit Settings > Developer

steps: Sign up at kit.com (formerly ConvertKit).
Go to Settings > Developer.
Copy your API Key and API Secret.
Paste both values below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: up to 10,000 subscribers. Creator: $25/mo.

services.name: Beehiiv
services.description: Publish newsletters, manage subscribers, track analytics, and run monetization and referral programs on Beehiiv.

configFields.label: API Key
configFields.helpText: Found in Beehiiv Settings > Integrations > API

configFields.label: Publication ID
configFields.helpText: Found in your Beehiiv dashboard URL or API settings

steps: Sign up at beehiiv.com and create your publication.
Go to Settings > Integrations > API.
Click Generate API Key.
Copy the API key and your publication ID.
Paste both values below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: up to 2,500 subscribers. Scale: $39/mo.

services.name: Lemon Squeezy
services.description: Sell digital products, manage subscriptions, handle tax compliance, and generate license keys through Lemon Squeezy.

configFields.label: API Key
configFields.helpText: Generate in Lemon Squeezy Settings > API

configFields.label: Store ID
configFields.helpText: Found in your Lemon Squeezy dashboard URL

steps: Sign up at lemonsqueezy.com and set up your store.
Go to Settings > API.
Click Create API Key and give it a name.
Copy the API key (shown only once).
Copy your Store ID from the dashboard URL.
Paste both values below.
setupGuide.pricing: 5% + 50c per transaction. No monthly fee.

services.name: Gumroad
services.description: Sell digital products, memberships, and courses. Track sales, customers, and analytics on Gumroad.

configFields.label: Access Token
configFields.helpText: Generate in Gumroad Settings > Advanced > Applications

steps: Sign up at gumroad.com.
Go to Settings > Advanced > Applications.
Click Generate access token.
Copy the access token.
Paste it below.
setupGuide.pricing: 10% flat fee per sale. No monthly fee.

services.name: QuickBooks Online
services.description: Manage accounting, invoicing, expense tracking, tax reports, and customer payments through QuickBooks Online.

configFields.label: Client ID
configFields.helpText: Found in your Intuit Developer Portal app settings

configFields.label: Realm ID (Company ID)
configFields.helpText: Found in your QuickBooks URL or app connection settings

steps: Sign up at developer.intuit.com.
Create a new app and select QuickBooks Online and Payments.
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from Keys & OAuth.
Set the redirect URI to http://localhost:3000/api/field-ops/callback/quickbooks.
Use the OAuth 2.0 Playground to connect to your QuickBooks company and get a refresh token.
Copy the Realm ID from the connection and paste all credentials below.
setupGuide.pricing: QuickBooks Online starts at $30/mo. API access included.

services.name: Twilio (SMS)
services.description: Send SMS messages, make voice calls, verify phone numbers, and manage messaging services through Twilio.

configFields.label: Account SID
configFields.helpText: Found on your Twilio Console dashboard

configFields.label: From Phone Number
configFields.helpText: Your Twilio phone number (must be purchased in Twilio Console)

steps: Sign up at twilio.com and verify your email and phone.
From the Console dashboard, copy your Account SID and Auth Token.
Buy a phone number in the Phone Numbers section.
Copy the phone number (with country code, e.g. +15551234567).
Paste all credentials below.
Trial accounts can only send to verified numbers. Upgrade for full access.
setupGuide.pricing: SMS: ~$0.0079/message. Phone numbers: ~$1.15/mo.

services.name: WhatsApp Business
services.description: Send customer messages, manage templates, share media, and handle interactive messages through the WhatsApp Business API.

configFields.label: Access Token
configFields.placeholder: Your WhatsApp Cloud API Access Token
configFields.helpText: Generated in the Meta Developer Portal

configFields.label: Phone Number ID
configFields.helpText: Found in WhatsApp > API Setup in the Meta Developer Portal

configFields.label: Business Account ID
configFields.helpText: Found in WhatsApp > API Setup in the Meta Developer Portal

steps: Go to developers.facebook.com and create a new Business app.
Add the WhatsApp product to your app.
In WhatsApp > API Setup, set up a test phone number or add your business phone.
Generate a permanent access token (System User token recommended for production).
Copy the Phone Number ID and Business Account ID from the API Setup page.
Paste all credentials below.
setupGuide.pricing: First 1,000 conversations/month free. Then ~$0.005-0.08 per conversation.

services.name: Intercom
services.description: Manage in-app messaging, customer support conversations, help center articles, and ticket workflows through Intercom.

configFields.label: Access Token
configFields.helpText: Generate in Intercom: Settings > Integrations > Developer Hub

configFields.label: App ID
configFields.helpText: Found in Settings > Installation

steps: Sign up at intercom.com and set up your workspace.
Go to Settings > Integrations > Developer Hub.
Click Create app (or use an existing one).
Generate an access token with required permissions.
Copy your App ID from Settings > Installation.
Paste the access token and App ID below.
setupGuide.pricing: Starter: $39/mo per seat. Grows with usage.

services.name: DocuSign
services.description: Send documents for e-signature, manage templates, track signing status, and automate document workflows.

configFields.label: Integration Key
configFields.helpText: Found in DocuSign Developer Portal under your app settings

configFields.label: Account ID
configFields.helpText: Found in DocuSign Admin > Account

steps: Go to developers.docusign.com and sign in.
Create a new Integration (app) in the Developer Portal.
Copy the Integration Key and generate a Secret Key.
Set the redirect URI to http://localhost:3000/api/field-ops/callback/docusign.
Use the OAuth 2.0 flow to get an access token.
Copy your Account ID from the Admin dashboard and paste all credentials below.
setupGuide.pricing: Personal: $10/mo. Standard: $25/mo. Business: $40/mo.

services.name: Medium
services.description: Publish articles, manage drafts, and manage publications and tags on Medium.

configFields.label: Integration Token
configFields.helpText: Generate in Medium Settings > Security and apps

configFields.label: Author ID
configFields.helpText: Retrieved via the /me API endpoint after authentication

steps: Log into medium.com.
Go to Settings > Security and apps > Integration tokens.
Enter a description and click Generate token.
Copy the integration token.
To get your Author ID, call the /me endpoint with your token.
Paste both values below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free to publish. Medium Partner Program for monetization.

services.name: Substack
services.description: Publish newsletter posts, manage drafts, track subscribers, and view analytics on Substack.

configFields.label: API Key
configFields.helpText: Found in Substack Settings > Developers

configFields.label: Publication URL
configFields.helpText: Your Substack publication URL

steps: Set up your Substack publication at substack.com.
Go to Settings > Developers.
Generate an API key.
Copy the API key and your publication URL.
Paste both values below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free publishing. Substack takes 10% of paid subscriptions.

services.name: Ghost (Self-hosted)
services.description: Create and manage posts, pages, members, tags, and newsletters on a Ghost publishing platform.

configFields.label: Ghost API URL
configFields.helpText: Your Ghost site URL

configFields.label: Admin API Key
configFields.helpText: Found in Ghost Admin > Settings > Integrations

configFields.label: Content API Key
configFields.helpText: Found in Ghost Admin > Settings > Integrations

steps: Set up a Ghost instance (self-hosted or Ghost Pro).
Log into your Ghost Admin panel.
Go to Settings > Integrations.
Click Add Custom Integration and name it (e.g., Mission Control).
Copy the Admin API Key and Content API Key.
Paste both keys and your Ghost site URL below.
setupGuide.pricing: Self-hosted: free. Ghost Pro: from $9/mo.

services.name: PostHog
services.description: Track product analytics events, manage feature flags, run experiments, and build dashboards with PostHog.

configFields.label: Project API Key
configFields.helpText: Found in PostHog Project Settings

configFields.label: Project ID
configFields.helpText: Found in PostHog Project Settings

configFields.label: PostHog Host
configFields.helpText: Default: https://app.posthog.com. Change for self-hosted instances.

steps: Sign up at posthog.com (or set up a self-hosted instance).
Go to Project Settings in the left sidebar.
Copy your Project API Key and Project ID.
If self-hosted, note your PostHog host URL.
Paste the values below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 1M events/month. Paid: $0.00031/event after.

services.name: Mixpanel
services.description: Track user analytics events, build funnels, measure retention, and manage user profiles with Mixpanel.

configFields.label: Project Token
configFields.helpText: Found in Mixpanel Project Settings

configFields.label: API Secret
configFields.helpText: Found in Mixpanel Project Settings

steps: Sign up at mixpanel.com and create a project.
Go to Project Settings (gear icon).
Copy the Project Token and API Secret.
Paste both values below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 20M events/month. Growth: from $20/mo.

services.name: Zapier (Webhooks)
services.description: Automate workflows, trigger Zaps via webhooks, transfer data between 6000+ apps, and build multi-step automations.

configFields.label: Webhook URL
configFields.helpText: Created when you set up a Webhooks trigger in a Zap

configFields.label: API Key
configFields.helpText: Optional. Used for REST Hook subscriptions and advanced automation.

steps: Sign up at zapier.com.
Create a new Zap with a Webhooks by Zapier trigger.
Select Catch Hook and copy the webhook URL.
Add actions to your Zap (e.g., send email, create spreadsheet row).
Turn on the Zap.
Paste the webhook URL below. Optionally add an API key for REST Hooks.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 100 tasks/month. Starter: $19.99/mo for 750 tasks.

services.name: Make (Integromat)
services.description: Build visual workflow automations, trigger scenarios via webhooks, map data between services, and schedule recurring tasks.

configFields.label: API Token
configFields.helpText: Found in Make: Profile > API Access

configFields.label: Team ID
configFields.helpText: Found in your Make organization settings

configFields.label: Webhook URL
configFields.helpText: Optional. Created when you add a Webhook module to a scenario.

steps: Sign up at make.com.
Go to Profile > API Access.
Click Create a token and give it a name.
Copy the API token.
Copy your Team ID from your organization settings.
Optionally set up a webhook module in a scenario and copy its URL.
Paste all values below.
setupGuide.pricing: Free: 1,000 operations/month. Core: $9/mo for 10,000.
